tools.name: dropyour_whoami
tools.description: Check how this session is authenticated: resolved tier (anonymous / free / pro) and its limits. Call this after configuring an account token (dyk_...) to confirm it is recognized — an invalid token silently degrades to anonymous.

tools.name: dropyour_publish
tools.description: Publish a self-contained HTML file to Dropyour. Returns a stable public URL (one origin per drop — same address across updates, browser data survives) and a managementToken. KEEP THE TOKEN: it is shown once and is required to replace, configure or delete the drop (with an account token, owned drops

tools.name: dropyour_publish_site
tools.description: Publish a MULTI-FILE site from a .zip (index.html + css/js/images), base64-encoded. Requires a Pro account token — your tier is anonymous. Same permanent URL; assets served under the drop. Max 25 MB, up to 50 files.

tools.name: dropyour_replace
tools.description: Replace the HTML content of an existing drop. The URL never changes (origin contract): visitors' browser data survives the update. With an account token, owned drops need no managementToken.

tools.name: dropyour_update_settings
tools.description: Change a drop's visibility (private = link-only + noindex, public = indexable), its expiration, or remove the "Hosted on Dropyour" badge (badgeHidden — Pro account token required). Switching to public triggers a stricter content re-scan and may be rejected. With an account token, owned drops need no

tools.name: dropyour_status
tools.description: Get a drop's status: URL, visibility, expiration, size, approximate view count. With an account token, owned drops need no managementToken.

tools.name: dropyour_delete
tools.description: Permanently delete a drop. Immediate and final — the URL is never reassigned. With an account token, owned drops need no managementToken.

tools.name: dropyour_list
tools.description: List your drops. With an account token: all LIVE drops attached to your account (across sessions and channels — manageable without managementToken). Anonymous: only the drops published during this MCP session. Management tokens are never stored server-side.

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "dropyour_whoami" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Check how this session is authenticated: resolved tier (anonymous / free / pro) and its limits. Call this after configuring an account token (dyk_...) to confir…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: dropyour_status · Private data: dropyour_whoami, dropyour_publish, dropyour_publish_site, dropyour_replace, dropyour_update_settings, dropyour_status, dropyour_delete, dropyour_list · Public sink: dropyour_publish, dropyour_publish_site · Destructive: dropyour_publish, dropyour_publish_site, dropyour_replace, dropyour_update_settings, dropyour_status, dropyour_delete.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: dropyour_status · Private data: dropyour_whoami, dropyour_publish, dropyour_publish_site, dropyour_replace, dropyour_update_settings, dropyour_status, dropyour_delete, dropyour_list · Public sink: dropyour_publish, dropyour_publish_site · Destructive: dropyour_publish, dropyour_publish_site, dropyour_replace, dropyour_update_settings, dropyour_status, dropyour_delete

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
